backlog.title: Crate Naming Reconciliation
backlog.description: Align all source-code and documentation references to the hyphenated crate (`qmk-notifier`) / underscored firmware (`qmk_notifier`) naming contract established in the current PRD. This is a naming-only delta on the fully-implemented v0.3.0 crate (72 tests passing) — no functional change to wire protocol, public API, cache, burst-write, or reply parsing. The only genuinely outstanding code edits are mislabeled comment references discovered by a comprehensive naming audit (architecture/naming_audit.md).

milestones.title: Consistent naming across source and documentation
milestones.description: Fix all mislabeled firmware/binary references in source comments, optionally align cosmetic lib.rs naming, and run the final documentation grep gate to confirm the naming reconciliation is complete.

tasks.title: Fix mislabeled firmware-commit and binary-invocation references in source comments
tasks.description: The naming audit (architecture/naming_audit.md) found 3 comment-only references that name the wrong ecosystem artifact. Two in src/core.rs attribute firmware commit `01a51935` to `qmk-notifier` (hyphen = Rust crate) when it belongs to `qmk_notifier` (underscore = firmware repo, per PRD.md §4.4 line 210). One in src/main.rs shows `qmk_notifier` as the CLI binary name when the binary is `qmk-notifier`. All three are comment-only edits with zero runtime/compile impact.

subtasks.title: Fix two core.rs firmware-commit comment references (qmk-notifier → qmk_notifier)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: architecture/naming_audit.md rows `src/core.rs:129` and `src/core.rs:425` classify these as FIRMWARE references that currently use the CRATE name (hyphen). PRD.md §4.4 (h3.5, line ~210) writes the authoritative form: `fixed in qmk_notifier commit 01a51935` (underscore = firmware). Commit `01a51935` fixed the RAW reply size from 30→32 bytes — a firmware-side change that lives in `dabstractor/qmk_notifier`, NOT `dabstractor/qmk-notifier`.
2. INPUT: No data dependencies — these are standalone comment edits. Read `src/core.rs` to find the exact lines (search for `qmk-notifier commit` or `01a51935`).
3. LOGIC: In `src/core.rs`, replace the two instances of `qmk-notifier commit \`01a51935\`` with `qmk_notifier commit \`01a51935\``. These are the comment at approximately line 129 (`/// sends a 32-byte reply per report (fixed in qmk-notifier commit \`01a51935\`,`) and line 425 (`// valid 32-byte reply per report (qmk-notifier commit \`01a51935\`, which`). The word `qmk-notifier` in these specific comments refers to the firmware C module and must be `qmk_notifier` (underscore). Do NOT change any other `qmk-notifier` references in the file (e.g. those referring to this crate). Do NOT change any `use qmk_notifier::` / `mod qmk_notifier` imports — those are Cargo-derived library identifiers and are invariant.
4. OUTPUT: The two comments now correctly attribute commit `01a51935` to the firmware repo `qmk_notifier`. `cargo build` and `cargo test` (72 tests) must still pass — these are comment-only changes.
5. DOCS: none — inline comment edits only. No user-facing/config/API surface change.

subtasks.title: Fix main.rs binary-invocation comment reference (qmk_notifier → qmk-notifier)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: architecture/naming_audit.md row `src/main.rs:88` classifies this as a CRATE (binary) reference that currently uses the FIRMWARE name (underscore). The comment text reads `(e.g. \`qmk_notifier --list | head -1\`)` — this is a shell example invoking the CLI binary, which is named `qmk-notifier` (hyphen), not the firmware module.
2. INPUT: No data dependencies — standalone comment edit. Read `src/main.rs` around line 88 (the `reset_sigpipe_to_default` doc comment).
3. LOGIC: In `src/main.rs`, change the binary-invocation example in the SIGPIPE comment from `qmk_notifier --list | head -1` to `qmk-notifier --list | head -1`. This is in the doc comment for `reset_sigpipe_to_default` (approximately line 88). The binary IS `qmk-notifier` (per Cargo.toml `name = "qmk-notifier"` and PRD §2). Do NOT change the `use qmk_notifier::` import at line 1 — that is the Cargo-derived library identifier and is invariant.
4. OUTPUT: The SIGPIPE comment now correctly names the binary `qmk-notifier`. `cargo build` and `cargo test` must still pass.
5. DOCS: none — inline comment edit only.

tasks.title: Optional: Align lib.rs cosmetic naming (test argv[0] strings and doc comment)
tasks.description: The naming audit found 10 test argv[0] strings and 1 doc comment in src/lib.rs that use the library identifier `qmk_notifier` where the binary name `qmk-notifier` would be more precise for clap test diagnostics. clap derives its display `bin_name` from argv[0], so test failure output currently renders `qmk_notifier` instead of the real binary `qmk-notifier`. These changes are purely cosmetic — they affect no parsing logic, no pass/fail outcomes, and no production behavior. Per delta_prd.md, these are 'Optional cosmetic polish (not required for correctness).'

subtasks.title: Update 10 lib.rs test argv[0] strings from qmk_notifier to qmk-notifier + align doc comment
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: architecture/naming_audit.md identifies 10 ARGV0-class references in src/lib.rs test code (lines ~876, 887, 899, 912, 915, 924, 925, 926, 927, 944) and 1 ambiguous doc comment (line ~154). All use `"qmk_notifier"` as the argv[0] first element passed to clap's `try_get_matches_from`. clap uses argv[0] only for the display `bin_name` in help/usage/error text — it does not affect argument parsing. The actual binary is `qmk-notifier` (Cargo.toml `name = "qmk-notifier"`).
2. INPUT: No data dependencies. Read `src/lib.rs` and find all occurrences of `"qmk_notifier"` used as the first element in test arrays passed to `cli_for()` or `try_get_matches_from()`.
3. LOGIC: Replace the argv[0] string `"qmk_notifier"` with `"qmk-notifier"` in all 10 test sites. These appear as the first element of arrays like `&["qmk_notifier", "--query-info"]` → `&["qmk-notifier", "--query-info"]`. Also optionally update the doc comment at line ~154 (`/// Build the clap \`Command\` for qmk_notifier`) to use `qmk-notifier` if the intent is 'the binary'. Do NOT change any `use qmk_notifier::` or `mod qmk_notifier` references — those are Cargo-derived library identifiers (invariant). Do NOT change `core::` or `error::` module references. After editing, run `cargo test` — all 72 tests MUST still pass (these changes affect only argv[0] display strings, not parsing logic).
4. OUTPUT: Test failure/error output now renders the real binary name `qmk-notifier`. All 72 tests still pass. `cargo build` clean.
5. DOCS: none — test-internal cosmetic edits, no user-facing/config/API surface change.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: Final Mode B documentation sweep: confirm that README.md and PRD.md ecosystem references are consistent with the naming reconciliation, verify the Cargo.toml dependency list matches actual source usage, and document any PRD §2 dep-list drift that cannot be fixed (PRD.md is read-only). This task depends on all implementing subtasks so it runs last as the catch-all that prevents a coherent delta from shipping with stale docs.

subtasks.title: Final grep gate: confirm no stale naming references in README.md and PRD.md
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: architecture/naming_audit.md already classified all references in README.md and PRD.md as CORRECT (~46 references). This subtask is the final verification gate after source edits (T1, T2) are applied. The naming contract: `qmk-notifier` (hyphen) = Rust crate/package/repo/binary; `qmk_notifier` (underscore) = firmware C module/repo; `use qmk_notifier::` = Rust library identifier (Cargo-derived, invariant).
2. INPUT: Depends on P1.M1.T1.S1, P1.M1.T1.S2, P1.M1.T2.S1 (all source naming fixes applied).
3. LOGIC: Run `grep -rn 'qmk.notifier\|qmk_notifier\|qmk-notifier' README.md PRD.md Cargo.toml` and verify every reference matches its classification: crate/package/repo/binary references are `qmk-notifier` (hyphen); firmware references are `qmk_notifier` (underscore); library-id references (`use qmk_notifier::`) stay underscore. Confirm no stale `dabstractor/qmk_notifier` repo URLs appear where `dabstractor/qmk-notifier` is intended (and vice versa). If any stale reference is found, fix it. Verify `README.md` contains the library-identifier clarification (PRD §1.2: 'the import is `use qmk_notifier::` because Cargo derives `_` from `-`'). Confirm the `qmkonnect/Cargo.toml` consumer-pin example in PRD §13 shows `{ package = "qmk-notifier", git = ".../qmk-notifier", tag = "v0.3.0" }`.
4. OUTPUT: Grep gate passes — zero stale naming references in README.md or PRD.md. All references match the naming contract.
5. DOCS: This IS the Mode B documentation sync subtask. If any README.md/PRD.md reference needed fixing, it was fixed here.

subtasks.title: Verify Cargo.toml dependency list vs PRD §2 and document any drift
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: architecture/cargo_naming_convention.md and system_context.md document that PRD §2 lists `dirs = "5.0.1"` and says `toml`/`serde` are 'currently listed but unused.' The actual Cargo.toml has: `hidapi = "2.4.1"`, `clap = "4.5.31"`, `libc = "0.2"` (unix target). No `dirs`, no `toml`, no `serde`. The PRD §2 text is stale — `dirs` was removed when config-file support was dropped, `toml`/`serde` were removed entirely, and `libc` was added for the SIGPIPE fix. PRD.md is READ-ONLY per orchestrator constraints and MUST NOT be edited.
2. INPUT: Depends on P1.M1.T3.S1 (docs grep gate complete). Read current Cargo.toml and PRD.md §2.
3. LOGIC: (a) Verify Cargo.toml dependencies are correct for the actual source usage: `hidapi` (core.rs HID I/O), `clap` (lib.rs CLI), `libc` (main.rs SIGPIPE, unix-only). Confirm NO unused dependencies remain (no `dirs`, `toml`, `serde`). (b) Run `cargo build` and `cargo test` to confirm the dependency set compiles and all 72 tests pass. (c) Document the PRD §2 dep-list drift in the architecture findings (already done in system_context.md §'PRD §2 Dependency-List Drift'). Do NOT edit PRD.md — it is read-only. If README.md has a dependencies section, verify it matches Cargo.toml.
4. OUTPUT: Cargo.toml confirmed correct (no dead deps, all deps used). PRD §2 drift documented in architecture/system_context.md. `cargo build` clean, `cargo test` 72 passed.
5. DOCS: Verify README.md dependency info (if any) matches Cargo.toml. Architecture docs already document the PRD §2 drift. No PRD.md edits (read-only).
